1.arabic: بسم الله الرحمن الرحيم طس ۚ تلك آيات القرآن وكتاب مبين
1.english: Ta, Seen. These are the verses of the Qur'an and a clear Book
1.transliteration: Taa-Seeen; tilka Aayaatul Qur-aani wa Kitaabim Mubeen

1.arabic: هدى وبشرى للمؤمنين
1.english: As guidance and good tidings for the believers
1.transliteration: Hudanw wa bushraa lil mu'mineen

1.arabic: الذين يقيمون الصلاة ويؤتون الزكاة وهم بالآخرة هم يوقنون
1.english: Who establish prayer and give zakah, and of the Hereafter they are certain [in faith].
1.transliteration: Allazeena yuqeemoonas Salaata wa yu'toonaz Zakaata wa hum bil Aakhirati hum yooqinoon

1.arabic: إن الذين لا يؤمنون بالآخرة زينا لهم أعمالهم فهم يعمهون
1.english: Indeed, for those who do not believe in the Hereafter, We have made pleasing to them their deeds, so they wander blindly.
1.transliteration: Innal lazeena laa yu'mimoona bil Aakhirati zaiyannaa lahum a'maalahum fahum ya'mahoon

1.arabic: أولئك الذين لهم سوء العذاب وهم في الآخرة هم الأخسرون
1.english: Those are the ones for whom there will be the worst of punishment, and in the Hereafter they are the greatest losers.
1.transliteration: Ulaaa'ikal lazeena lahum sooo'ul 'azaabi wa hum fil Aakhirati humul akhsaroon

1.arabic: وإنك لتلقى القرآن من لدن حكيم عليم
1.english: And indeed, [O Muhammad], you receive the Qur'an from one Wise and Knowing.
1.transliteration: Wa innaka latulaqqal Qur-aana mil ladun Hakeemin 'Aleem

1.arabic: إذ قال موسى لأهله إني آنست نارا سآتيكم منها بخبر أو آتيكم بشهاب قبس لعلكم تصطلون
1.english: [Mention] when Moses said to his family, "Indeed, I have perceived a fire. I will bring you from there information or will bring you a burning torch that you may warm yourselves."
1.transliteration: Iz qaala Moosaa li ahliheee inneee aanastu naaran saaateekum minhaa bikhabarin aw aateekum bishihaabin qabasil la'allakum tastaloon

1.arabic: فلما جاءها نودي أن بورك من في النار ومن حولها وسبحان الله رب العالمين
1.english: But when he came to it, he was called, "Blessed is whoever is at the fire and whoever is around it. And exalted is Allah, Lord of the worlds.
1.transliteration: Falammaa jaaa'ahaa noodiya am boorika man finnnnaari wa man hawlahaa wa Subhaanal laahi Rabbil 'aalameen

1.arabic: يا موسى إنه أنا الله العزيز الحكيم
1.english: O Moses, indeed it is I - Allah, the Exalted in Might, the Wise."
1.transliteration: Yaa Moosaaa innahooo Anal laahul 'Azeezul Hakeem

1.arabic: وألق عصاك ۚ فلما رآها تهتز كأنها جان ولى مدبرا ولم يعقب ۚ يا موسى لا تخف إني لا يخاف لدي المرسلون
1.english: And [he was told], "Throw down your staff." But when he saw it writhing as if it were a snake, he turned in flight and did not return. [Allah said], "O Moses, fear not. Indeed, in My presence the messengers do not fear.
1.transliteration: Wa alqi 'asaak; falammmaa ra aahaa tahtazzu ka annahaa jaaannunw wallaa mudbiranw wa lam yu'aqqib; yaa Moosaa laa takhaf innee laa yakhaafu ladaiyal mursaloon

1.arabic: إلا من ظلم ثم بدل حسنا بعد سوء فإني غفور رحيم
1.english: Otherwise, he who wrongs, then substitutes good after evil - indeed, I am Forgiving and Merciful.
1.transliteration: Illaa man zalama summa baddala husnam ba'da sooo'in fa innee Ghafoorur Raheem

1.arabic: وأدخل يدك في جيبك تخرج بيضاء من غير سوء ۖ في تسع آيات إلى فرعون وقومه ۚ إنهم كانوا قوما فاسقين
1.english: And put your hand into the opening of your garment [at the breast]; it will come out white without disease. [These are] among the nine signs [you will take] to Pharaoh and his people. Indeed, they have been a people defiantly disobedient."
1.transliteration: Wa adkhil yadaka fee jaibika takhruj baidaaa'a min ghairisooo'in feetis'i Aayaatin ilaa Fir'awna wa qawmih; innahum kaanoo qawman faasiqeen

1.arabic: فلما جاءتهم آياتنا مبصرة قالوا هذا سحر مبين
1.english: But when there came to them Our visible signs, they said, "This is obvious magic."
1.transliteration: Falammaa jaaa'at hum Aayaatunaa mubsiratan qaaloo haazaa sihrum mubeen

1.arabic: وجحدوا بها واستيقنتها أنفسهم ظلما وعلوا ۚ فانظر كيف كان عاقبة المفسدين
1.english: And they rejected them, while their [inner] selves were convinced thereof, out of injustice and haughtiness. So see how was the end of the corrupters.
1.transliteration: Wa jahadoo bihaa wastaiqanat haaa anfusuhum zulmanw-wa 'uluwwaa; fanzur kaifa kaana 'aaqibatul mufsideen

1.arabic: ولقد آتينا داوود وسليمان علما ۖ وقالا الحمد لله الذي فضلنا على كثير من عباده المؤمنين
1.english: And We had certainly given to David and Solomon knowledge, and they said, "Praise [is due] to Allah, who has favored us over many of His believing servants."
1.transliteration: Wa laqad aatainaa Daawooda wa sulaimaana 'ilmaa; wa qaalal hamdu lil laahil lazee faddalanaa 'alaa kaseerim min 'ibaadihil mu'mineen

1.arabic: وورث سليمان داوود ۖ وقال يا أيها الناس علمنا منطق الطير وأوتينا من كل شيء ۖ إن هذا لهو الفضل المبين
1.english: And Solomon inherited David. He said, "O people, we have been taught the language of birds, and we have been given from all things. Indeed, this is evident bounty."
1.transliteration: Wa warisa Sulaimaanu Daawooda wa qaala yaaa aiyuhan naasu 'ullimnaa mantiqat tairi wa ooteenaa min kulli shai'in inna haazaa lahuwal fadlul mubeen

1.arabic: وحشر لسليمان جنوده من الجن والإنس والطير فهم يوزعون
1.english: And gathered for Solomon were his soldiers of the jinn and men and birds, and they were [marching] in rows.
1.transliteration: Wa hushira Sulaimaana junooduhoo minal jinni wal insi wattairi fahum yooza'oon

1.arabic: حتى إذا أتوا على واد النمل قالت نملة يا أيها النمل ادخلوا مساكنكم لا يحطمنكم سليمان وجنوده وهم لا يشعرون
1.english: Until, when they came upon the valley of the ants, an ant said, "O ants, enter your dwellings that you not be crushed by Solomon and his soldiers while they perceive not."
1.transliteration: hattaaa izaaa ataw 'alaa waadin namli qaalat namlatuny yaaa aiyuhan namlud khuloo masaakinakum laa yahtimannakum Sulaimaanu wa junooduhoo wa hum laa yash'uroon

1.arabic: فتبسم ضاحكا من قولها وقال رب أوزعني أن أشكر نعمتك التي أنعمت علي وعلى والدي وأن أعمل صالحا ترضاه وأدخلني برحمتك في عبادك الصالحين
1.english: So [Solomon] smiled, amused at her speech, and said, "My Lord, enable me to be grateful for Your favor which You have bestowed upon me and upon my parents and to do righteousness of which You approve. And admit me by Your mercy into [the ranks of] Your righteous servants."
1.transliteration: Fatabassama daahikam min qawlihaa wa qaala Rabbi awzi'nee an ashkura ni'mata kal lateee an'amta 'alaiya wa 'alaa waalidaiya wa an a'mala saalihan tardaahu wa adkhilnee birahmatika fee 'ibaadikas saaliheen

1.arabic: وتفقد الطير فقال ما لي لا أرى الهدهد أم كان من الغائبين
1.english: And he took attendance of the birds and said, "Why do I not see the hoopoe - or is he among the absent?
1.transliteration: Wa tafaqqadat taira faqaala maa liya laaa aral hud hud, am kaana minal ghaaa'ibeen

1.arabic: لأعذبنه عذابا شديدا أو لأذبحنه أو ليأتيني بسلطان مبين
1.english: I will surely punish him with a severe punishment or slaughter him unless he brings me clear authorization."
1.transliteration: La-u'azzibanahoo 'azaaban shadeedan aw la azbahannahoo aw layaatiyannee bisultaanim mubeen

1.arabic: فمكث غير بعيد فقال أحطت بما لم تحط به وجئتك من سبإ بنبإ يقين
1.english: But the hoopoe stayed not long and said, "I have encompassed [in knowledge] that which you have not encompassed, and I have come to you from Sheba with certain news.
1.transliteration: Famakasa ghaira ba'eedin faqaala ahattu bimaa lam tuhit bihee wa ji'tuka min Sabaim binaba iny-yaqeen

1.arabic: إني وجدت امرأة تملكهم وأوتيت من كل شيء ولها عرش عظيم
1.english: Indeed, I found [there] a woman ruling them, and she has been given of all things, and she has a great throne.
1.transliteration: Innee wajattum ra atan tamlikuhum wa ootiyat min kulli shai'inw wa lahaa 'arshun 'azeem

1.arabic: وجدتها وقومها يسجدون للشمس من دون الله وزين لهم الشيطان أعمالهم فصدهم عن السبيل فهم لا يهتدون
1.english: I found her and her people prostrating to the sun instead of Allah, and Satan has made their deeds pleasing to them and averted them from [His] way, so they are not guided,
1.transliteration: Wajattuhaa wa qawmahaa yasjudoona lishshamsi min doonil laahi wa zaiyana lahumush Shaitaanu a'maalahum fasaddahum 'anis sabeeli fahum laa yahtadoon

1.arabic: ألا يسجدوا لله الذي يخرج الخبء في السماوات والأرض ويعلم ما تخفون وما تعلنون
1.english: [And] so they do not prostrate to Allah, who brings forth what is hidden within the heavens and the earth and knows what you conceal and what you declare -
1.transliteration: Allaa yasjudoo lillaahil lazee yukhrijul khab'a fis samaawaati wal ardi wa ya'lamu maa tukhfoona wa maa tu'linoon

1.arabic: الله لا إله إلا هو رب العرش العظيم ۩
1.english: Allah - there is no deity except Him, Lord of the Great Throne."
1.transliteration: Allaahu laaa ilaaha illaa Huwa Rabbul 'Arshil Azeem

1.arabic: ۞ قال سننظر أصدقت أم كنت من الكاذبين
1.english: [Solomon] said, "We will see whether you were truthful or were of the liars.
1.transliteration: Qaala sananzuru asadaqta am kunta minal kaazibeen

1.arabic: اذهب بكتابي هذا فألقه إليهم ثم تول عنهم فانظر ماذا يرجعون
1.english: Take this letter of mine and deliver it to them. Then leave them and see what [answer] they will return."
1.transliteration: Izhab bikitaabee haaza fa alqih ilaihim summma tawalla 'anhum fanzur maazaa yarji'oon

1.arabic: قالت يا أيها الملأ إني ألقي إلي كتاب كريم
1.english: She said, "O eminent ones, indeed, to me has been delivered a noble letter.
1.transliteration: Qaalat yaaa aiyuhal mala'u innee ulqiya ilaiya kitaabun kareem

1.arabic: إنه من سليمان وإنه بسم الله الرحمن الرحيم
1.english: Indeed, it is from Solomon, and indeed, it reads: 'In the name of Allah, the Entirely Merciful, the Especially Merciful,
1.transliteration: Innahoo min Sulaimaana wa innahoo bismil laahir Rahmaanir Raheem

1.arabic: ألا تعلوا علي وأتوني مسلمين
1.english: Be not haughty with me but come to me in submission [as Muslims].' "
1.transliteration: Allaa ta'loo 'alaiya waa toonee muslimeen

1.arabic: قالت يا أيها الملأ أفتوني في أمري ما كنت قاطعة أمرا حتى تشهدون
1.english: She said, "O eminent ones, advise me in my affair. I would not decide a matter until you witness [for] me."
1.transliteration: Qaalat yaaa aiyuhal mala'u aftoonee fee amree maa kuntu qaati'atan amran hattaa tashhhaddon

1.arabic: قالوا نحن أولو قوة وأولو بأس شديد والأمر إليك فانظري ماذا تأمرين
1.english: They said, "We are men of strength and of great military might, but the command is yours, so see what you will command."
1.transliteration: Qaaloo nahnu uloo quwwatinw wa uloo baasin shadeed; wal amru ilaiki fanzuree maazaa taamureen

1.arabic: قالت إن الملوك إذا دخلوا قرية أفسدوها وجعلوا أعزة أهلها أذلة ۖ وكذلك يفعلون
1.english: She said, "Indeed kings - when they enter a city, they ruin it and render the honored of its people humbled. And thus do they do.
1.transliteration: Qaalat innal mulooka izaa dakhaloo qaryatan afsadoohaa wa ja'alooo a'izzata ahlihaaa azillah; wa kazaalika yaf'aloon

1.arabic: وإني مرسلة إليهم بهدية فناظرة بم يرجع المرسلون
1.english: But indeed, I will send to them a gift and see with what [reply] the messengers will return."
1.transliteration: Wa innee mursilatun ilaihim bihadiyyatin fanaaziratum bima yarji'ul mursaloon

1.arabic: فلما جاء سليمان قال أتمدونن بمال فما آتاني الله خير مما آتاكم بل أنتم بهديتكم تفرحون
1.english: So when they came to Solomon, he said, "Do you provide me with wealth? But what Allah has given me is better than what He has given you. Rather, it is you who rejoice in your gift.
1.transliteration: Falammaa jaaa'a Sulaimaana qaala atumiddoonani bimaalin famaaa aataakum bal antum bihadiy yatikum tafrahoon

1.arabic: ارجع إليهم فلنأتينهم بجنود لا قبل لهم بها ولنخرجنهم منها أذلة وهم صاغرون
1.english: Return to them, for we will surely come to them with soldiers that they will be powerless to encounter, and we will surely expel them therefrom in humiliation, and they will be debased."
1.transliteration: Irji' ilaihim falanaatiyan nahum bijunoodil laa qibala lahum bihaa wa lanukhri jannahum minhaaa azillatanw wa hum saaghiroon

1.arabic: قال يا أيها الملأ أيكم يأتيني بعرشها قبل أن يأتوني مسلمين
1.english: [Solomon] said, "O assembly [of jinn], which of you will bring me her throne before they come to me in submission?"
1.transliteration: Qaala yaaa aiyuhal mala'u aiyukum yaateenee bi'arshihaa qabla ai yaatoonee muslimeen

1.arabic: قال عفريت من الجن أنا آتيك به قبل أن تقوم من مقامك ۖ وإني عليه لقوي أمين
1.english: A powerful one from among the jinn said, "I will bring it to you before you rise from your place, and indeed, I am for this [task] strong and trustworthy."
1.transliteration: Qaala 'ifreetum minal jinni ana aateeka bihee qabla an taqooma mim maqaamika wa innee 'alaihi laqawiyyun ameen

1.arabic: قال الذي عنده علم من الكتاب أنا آتيك به قبل أن يرتد إليك طرفك ۚ فلما رآه مستقرا عنده قال هذا من فضل ربي ليبلوني أأشكر أم أكفر ۖ ومن شكر فإنما يشكر لنفسه ۖ ومن كفر فإن ربي غني كريم
1.english: Said one who had knowledge from the Scripture, "I will bring it to you before your glance returns to you." And when [Solomon] saw it placed before him, he said, "This is from the favor of my Lord to test me whether I will be grateful or ungrateful. And whoever is grateful - his gratitude is only for [the benefit of] himself. And whoever is ungrateful - then indeed, my Lord is Free of need and Generous."
1.transliteration: Qaalal lazee indahoo 'ilmum minal Kitaabi ana aateeka bihee qabla ai yartadda ilaika tarfuk; falammaa ra aahu mustaqirran 'indahoo qaala haazaa min fadli Rabbee li yabluwaneee 'a-ashkuru am akfuru wa man shakara fa innamaa yashkuru linafsihee wa man kafara fa inna Rabbee Ghaniyyun Kareem

1.arabic: قال نكروا لها عرشها ننظر أتهتدي أم تكون من الذين لا يهتدون
1.english: He said, "Disguise for her her throne; we will see whether she will be guided [to truth] or will be of those who is not guided."
1.transliteration: Qaala nakkiroo lahaa 'arshahaa nanzur atahtadeee am takoonu minal lazeena laa yahtadoon

1.arabic: فلما جاءت قيل أهكذا عرشك ۖ قالت كأنه هو ۚ وأوتينا العلم من قبلها وكنا مسلمين
1.english: So when she arrived, it was said [to her], "Is your throne like this?" She said, "[It is] as though it was it." [Solomon said], "And we were given knowledge before her, and we have been Muslims [in submission to Allah].
1.transliteration: Falammaa jaaa'at qeela ahaakaza 'arshuki qaalat kaanna hoo; wa ooteenal 'ilma min qablihaa wa kunnaa muslimeen

1.arabic: وصدها ما كانت تعبد من دون الله ۖ إنها كانت من قوم كافرين
1.english: And that which she was worshipping other than Allah had averted her [from submission to Him]. Indeed, she was from a disbelieving people."
1.transliteration: Wa saddahaa maa kaanat ta'budu min doonil laahi innahaa kaanat min qawmin kaafireen

1.arabic: قيل لها ادخلي الصرح ۖ فلما رأته حسبته لجة وكشفت عن ساقيها ۚ قال إنه صرح ممرد من قوارير ۗ قالت رب إني ظلمت نفسي وأسلمت مع سليمان لله رب العالمين
1.english: She was told, "Enter the palace." But when she saw it, she thought it was a body of water and uncovered her shins [to wade through]. He said, "Indeed, it is a palace [whose floor is] made smooth with glass." She said, "My Lord, indeed I have wronged myself, and I submit with Solomon to Allah, Lord of the worlds."
1.transliteration: Qeela lahad khulis sarha falammaa ra at hu hasibat hu lujjatanw wa khashafat 'an saaqaihaa; qaala innahoo sarhum mumarradum min qawaareer; qaalat Rabbi innee zalamtu nafsee wa aslamtu ma'a Sulaimaana lillaahi Rabbil 'aalameen

1.arabic: ولقد أرسلنا إلى ثمود أخاهم صالحا أن اعبدوا الله فإذا هم فريقان يختصمون
1.english: And We had certainly sent to Thamud their brother Salih, [saying], "Worship Allah," and at once they were two parties conflicting.
1.transliteration: Wa laqad arsalnaaa ilaa Samoda akhaahum Saalihan ani'budul lahha fa izaa hum fareeqaani yakhtasimoon

1.arabic: قال يا قوم لم تستعجلون بالسيئة قبل الحسنة ۖ لولا تستغفرون الله لعلكم ترحمون
1.english: He said, "O my people, why are you impatient for evil instead of good? Why do you not seek forgiveness of Allah that you may receive mercy?"
1.transliteration: Qaala yaa qawmi lima tasta'jiloona bissaiyi'ati qablal hasanati law laa tas taghfiroonal laaha la'allakum turhamoon

1.arabic: قالوا اطيرنا بك وبمن معك ۚ قال طائركم عند الله ۖ بل أنتم قوم تفتنون
1.english: They said, "We consider you a bad omen, you and those with you." He said, "Your omen is with Allah. Rather, you are a people being tested."
1.transliteration: Qaalut taiyarnaa bika wa bimam ma'ak; qaala taaa'irukum 'indal laahi bal antum qawmun tuftanoon

1.arabic: وكان في المدينة تسعة رهط يفسدون في الأرض ولا يصلحون
1.english: And there were in the city nine family heads causing corruption in the land and not amending [its affairs].
1.transliteration: Wa kaana fil madeenati tis'atu rahtiny yufsidoona fil ardi wa laa yuslihoon

1.arabic: قالوا تقاسموا بالله لنبيتنه وأهله ثم لنقولن لوليه ما شهدنا مهلك أهله وإنا لصادقون
1.english: They said, "Take a mutual oath by Allah that we will kill him by night, he and his family. Then we will say to his executor, 'We did not witness the destruction of his family, and indeed, we are truthful.' "
1.transliteration: Qaaloo taqaasamoo billaahi lanubaiyitannahoo wa ahlahoo summaa lanaqoolana liwaliy yihee maa shahidnaa mahlika ahliee wa innaa lasaadiqoon

1.arabic: ومكروا مكرا ومكرنا مكرا وهم لا يشعرون
1.english: And they planned a plan, and We planned a plan, while they perceived not.
1.transliteration: Wa makaroo makranw wa makarnaa makranw wa hum laa yash'uroon

1.arabic: فانظر كيف كان عاقبة مكرهم أنا دمرناهم وقومهم أجمعين
1.english: Then look how was the outcome of their plan - that We destroyed them and their people, all.
1.transliteration: Fanzur kaifa kaana 'aaqibatu makrihim annaa dammar naahum wa qawmahum ajma'een

1.arabic: فتلك بيوتهم خاوية بما ظلموا ۗ إن في ذلك لآية لقوم يعلمون
1.english: So those are their houses, desolate because of the wrong they had done. Indeed in that is a sign for people who know.
1.transliteration: Fatilka buyootuhum khaa wiyatam bimaa zalamoo; inna fee zaalika la Aayatal liqaw miny-ya'lamoon

1.arabic: وأنجينا الذين آمنوا وكانوا يتقون
1.english: And We saved those who believed and used to fear Allah.
1.transliteration: Wa anjainal lazeena aamanoo wa kaanoo yattaqoon

1.arabic: ولوطا إذ قال لقومه أتأتون الفاحشة وأنتم تبصرون
1.english: And [mention] Lot, when he said to his people, "Do you commit immorality while you are seeing?
1.transliteration: Wa lootan iz qaala liqawmiheee ataatoonal faa hishata wa antum qawmun tajjhaloon

1.arabic: أئنكم لتأتون الرجال شهوة من دون النساء ۚ بل أنتم قوم تجهلون
1.english: Do you indeed approach men with desire instead of women? Rather, you are a people behaving ignorantly."
1.transliteration: A'innakum lataatoonar rijaala shahwatam min doonin nisaaa'; bal antum qawmun tajhaloon

1.arabic: ۞ فما كان جواب قومه إلا أن قالوا أخرجوا آل لوط من قريتكم ۖ إنهم أناس يتطهرون
1.english: But the answer of his people was not except that they said, "Expel the family of Lot from your city. Indeed, they are people who keep themselves pure."
1.transliteration: Fammaa kaana jawaaba qawmiheee illaaa an qaalooo akrijoon aalaa Lootim min qaryatikum innahum unaasuny yatatahharoon

1.arabic: فأنجيناه وأهله إلا امرأته قدرناها من الغابرين
1.english: So We saved him and his family, except for his wife; We destined her to be of those who remained behind.
1.transliteration: Fa anjainaahu wa ahlahooo illam ra atahoo qaddarnaahaa minal ghaabireen

1.arabic: وأمطرنا عليهم مطرا ۖ فساء مطر المنذرين
1.english: And We rained upon them a rain [of stones], and evil was the rain of those who were warned.
1.transliteration: Wa amtarnaa 'alaihimm mataran fasaaa'a matarul munzareen

1.arabic: قل الحمد لله وسلام على عباده الذين اصطفى ۗ آلله خير أما يشركون
1.english: Say, [O Muhammad], "Praise be to Allah, and peace upon His servants whom He has chosen. Is Allah better or what they associate with Him?"
1.transliteration: Qulil hamdu lillaahi wa salaamun 'alaa 'ibaadihil lazeenas tafaa; aaallaahu khairun ammmaa yushrikoon

1.arabic: أمن خلق السماوات والأرض وأنزل لكم من السماء ماء فأنبتنا به حدائق ذات بهجة ما كان لكم أن تنبتوا شجرها ۗ أإله مع الله ۚ بل هم قوم يعدلون
1.english: [More precisely], is He [not best] who created the heavens and the earth and sent down for you rain from the sky, causing to grow thereby gardens of joyful beauty which you could not [otherwise] have grown the trees thereof? Is there a deity with Allah? [No], but they are a people who ascribe equals [to Him].
1.transliteration: Amman khalaqas samaawaati wal arda wa anzala lakum minas samaaa'i maaa'an fa ambatnaa bihee hadaaa'iqa zaata bahjjah; maa kanna lakum an tumbitoo shajarahaa; 'a-ilaahum ma'al laah; bal hum qawmuny ya'diloon

1.arabic: أمن جعل الأرض قرارا وجعل خلالها أنهارا وجعل لها رواسي وجعل بين البحرين حاجزا ۗ أإله مع الله ۚ بل أكثرهم لا يعلمون
1.english: Is He [not best] who made the earth a stable ground and placed within it rivers and made for it firmly set mountains and placed between the two seas a barrier? Is there a deity with Allah? [No], but most of them do not know.
1.transliteration: Ammann ja'alal arda qaraaranw wa ja'ala khilaalahaaa anhaaranw wa ja'ala lahaa rawaasiya wa ja'ala bainal bahraini haajizaa; 'a-ilaahumma'allah; bal aksaruhum la ya'lamoon

1.arabic: أمن يجيب المضطر إذا دعاه ويكشف السوء ويجعلكم خلفاء الأرض ۗ أإله مع الله ۚ قليلا ما تذكرون
1.english: Is He [not best] who responds to the desperate one when he calls upon Him and removes evil and makes you inheritors of the earth? Is there a deity with Allah? Little do you remember.
1.transliteration: Ammany-yujeebul mud tarra izaa da'aahu wa yakshifussooo'a wa yaj'alukum khula faaa'al ardi 'a-ilaahum ma'al laahi qaleelam maa tazak karoon

1.arabic: أمن يهديكم في ظلمات البر والبحر ومن يرسل الرياح بشرا بين يدي رحمته ۗ أإله مع الله ۚ تعالى الله عما يشركون
1.english: Is He [not best] who guides you through the darknesses of the land and sea and who sends the winds as good tidings before His mercy? Is there a deity with Allah? High is Allah above whatever they associate with Him.
1.transliteration: Ammany-yahdeekum fee zulumaatil barri wal bahri wa many yursilu riyaaha bushram baina yadai rahmatih; 'a-ilaahum ma'al laah; Ta'aalal laahu 'ammaa yushrikoon

1.arabic: أمن يبدأ الخلق ثم يعيده ومن يرزقكم من السماء والأرض ۗ أإله مع الله ۚ قل هاتوا برهانكم إن كنتم صادقين
1.english: Is He [not best] who begins creation and then repeats it and who provides for you from the heaven and earth? Is there a deity with Allah? Say, "Produce your proof, if you should be truthful."
1.transliteration: Ammmany-yabda'ul khalqa summa yu'eeduhoo wa many-yarzuqukum minas sammaaa'i wal ard; 'a-ilaahum ma'allah; qul haatoo burhaanakum in kuntum saadiqeen

1.arabic: قل لا يعلم من في السماوات والأرض الغيب إلا الله ۚ وما يشعرون أيان يبعثون
1.english: Say, "None in the heavens and earth knows the unseen except Allah, and they do not perceive when they will be resurrected."
1.transliteration: Qul laa ya'lamu mman fis sammaawaati wal ardil ghaiba illal laah; wa maa yash'uroona aiyaana yub'asoon

1.arabic: بل ادارك علمهم في الآخرة ۚ بل هم في شك منها ۖ بل هم منها عمون
1.english: Rather, their knowledge is arrested concerning the Hereafter. Rather, they are in doubt about it. Rather, they are, concerning it, blind.
1.transliteration: Balid daaraka 'ilmuhum fil Aakhirah; bal hum fee shakkim minhaa bal hum minhaa 'amoon

1.arabic: وقال الذين كفروا أإذا كنا ترابا وآباؤنا أئنا لمخرجون
1.english: And those who disbelieve say, "When we have become dust as well as our forefathers, will we indeed be brought out [of the graves]?
1.transliteration: Wa qaalal lazeena kafarooo 'a-izaa kunnaa turaabanw wa aabaaa'unaaa a'innaa lamukhrajoon

1.arabic: لقد وعدنا هذا نحن وآباؤنا من قبل إن هذا إلا أساطير الأولين
1.english: We have been promised this, we and our forefathers, before. This is not but legends of the former peoples."
1.transliteration: Laqad wu'idnaa haazaa nahnu wa aabaaa'unaa min qablu in haazaaa illaaa asaateerul awwaleen

1.arabic: قل سيروا في الأرض فانظروا كيف كان عاقبة المجرمين
1.english: Say, [O Muhammad], "Travel through the land and observe how was the end of the criminals."
1.transliteration: Qul seeroo fil ardi fanzuroo kaifa kaana 'aaqibatul mujremeen

1.arabic: ولا تحزن عليهم ولا تكن في ضيق مما يمكرون
1.english: And grieve not over them or be in distress from what they conspire.
1.transliteration: Wa laa tahzan 'alaihim wa laa takun fee daiqim mimmaa yamkuroon

1.arabic: ويقولون متى هذا الوعد إن كنتم صادقين
1.english: And they say, "When is [the fulfillment of] this promise, if you should be truthful?"
1.transliteration: Wa yaqooloona mataa haazal wa'du in kuntum saadiqeen

1.arabic: قل عسى أن يكون ردف لكم بعض الذي تستعجلون
1.english: Say, "Perhaps it is close behind you - some of that for which you are impatient.
1.transliteration: Qul 'asaaa any-yakoona radifa lakum ba'dul lazee tasta'jiloon

1.arabic: وإن ربك لذو فضل على الناس ولكن أكثرهم لا يشكرون
1.english: And indeed, your Lord is full of bounty for the people, but most of them do not show gratitude."
1.transliteration: Wa innna Rabbaka lazoo fadlin 'alan naasi wa laakina aksarahum laa yashkuroon

1.arabic: وإن ربك ليعلم ما تكن صدورهم وما يعلنون
1.english: And indeed, your Lord knows what their breasts conceal and what they declare.
1.transliteration: Wa inna Rabbaka la ya'lamu maa tukinnu sudooruhum wa maa yu'linoon

1.arabic: وما من غائبة في السماء والأرض إلا في كتاب مبين
1.english: And there is nothing concealed within the heaven and the earth except that it is in a clear Register.
1.transliteration: Wa maa min ghaaa'ibatin fis samaaa'i wal ardi illaa fee kitaabimm mubeen

1.arabic: إن هذا القرآن يقص على بني إسرائيل أكثر الذي هم فيه يختلفون
1.english: Indeed, this Qur'an relates to the Children of Israel most of that over which they disagree.
1.transliteration: Inna haazal Qur-aana yaqussu 'alaa Baneee israaa'eela aksaral lazee hum feehi yakhtalifoon

1.arabic: وإنه لهدى ورحمة للمؤمنين
1.english: And indeed, it is guidance and mercy for the believers.
1.transliteration: Wa innahoo lahudanw wa rahmatul lilmu'mineen

1.arabic: إن ربك يقضي بينهم بحكمه ۚ وهو العزيز العليم
1.english: Indeed, your Lord will judge between them by His [wise] judgement. And He is the Exalted in Might, the Knowing.
1.transliteration: Inna Rabbaka yaqdee bainahum bihukmih; wa Huwal 'Azeezul 'Aleem

1.arabic: فتوكل على الله ۖ إنك على الحق المبين
1.english: So rely upon Allah; indeed, you are upon the clear truth.
1.transliteration: Fatawakkal 'alal laahi innaka 'alal haqqil mubeen

1.arabic: إنك لا تسمع الموتى ولا تسمع الصم الدعاء إذا ولوا مدبرين
1.english: Indeed, you will not make the dead hear, nor will you make the deaf hear the call when they have turned their backs retreating.
1.transliteration: Innaka laa tusmi'ul mawtaa wa laa tusmi'us summad du'aaa izaa wallaw mudbireen

1.arabic: وما أنت بهادي العمي عن ضلالتهم ۖ إن تسمع إلا من يؤمن بآياتنا فهم مسلمون
1.english: And you cannot guide the blind away from their error. You will only make hear those who believe in Our verses so they are Muslims [submitting to Allah].
1.transliteration: Wa maaa anta bihaadil 'umyi 'an dalaalatihim in tusmi'u illaa mai yu'minu bi aayaatinaa fahum muslimoon

1.arabic: ۞ وإذا وقع القول عليهم أخرجنا لهم دابة من الأرض تكلمهم أن الناس كانوا بآياتنا لا يوقنون
1.english: And when the word befalls them, We will bring forth for them a creature from the earth speaking to them, [saying] that the people were, of Our verses, not certain [in faith].
1.transliteration: Wa izaa waqa'al qawhu 'alaihim akhrajnaa lahum daabbatam minal ardi tukal limuhum annan naasa kaanoo bi aayaatinaa laa yooqinoon

1.arabic: ويوم نحشر من كل أمة فوجا ممن يكذب بآياتنا فهم يوزعون
1.english: And [warn of] the Day when We will gather from every nation a company of those who deny Our signs, and they will be [driven] in rows
1.transliteration: Wa Yawma nahshuru min kulli ummmatin fawjam mim many-yukazzibu bi Aayaatinaa fahum yooza'oon

1.arabic: حتى إذا جاءوا قال أكذبتم بآياتي ولم تحيطوا بها علما أماذا كنتم تعملون
1.english: Until, when they arrive [at the place of Judgement], He will say, "Did you deny My signs while you encompassed them not in knowledge, or what [was it that] you were doing?"
1.transliteration: Hattaaa izaa jaaa'oo qaala akazzabtum bi Aayaatee wa lam tuheetoo bihaa 'ilman ammaazaa kuntum ta'maloon

1.arabic: ووقع القول عليهم بما ظلموا فهم لا ينطقون
1.english: And the decree will befall them for the wrong they did, and they will not [be able to] speak.
1.transliteration: Wa waqa'al qawlu 'alaihim bimaa zalamoo fahum laa yantiqoon

1.arabic: ألم يروا أنا جعلنا الليل ليسكنوا فيه والنهار مبصرا ۚ إن في ذلك لآيات لقوم يؤمنون
1.english: Do they not see that We made the night that they may rest therein and the day giving sight? Indeed in that are signs for a people who believe.
1.transliteration: Alam yaraw annaa ja'alnal laila li yaskunoo feehi wannahaara mubsiraa; inna fee zaalika la Aayaatil liqaw miny-yu'minoon

1.arabic: ويوم ينفخ في الصور ففزع من في السماوات ومن في الأرض إلا من شاء الله ۚ وكل أتوه داخرين
1.english: And [warn of] the Day the Horn will be blown, and whoever is in the heavens and whoever is on the earth will be terrified except whom Allah wills. And all will come to Him humbled.
1.transliteration: Wa Yawma yunfakhu fis Soori fafazi'a man fis samaawaati wa man fil ardi illaa man shaaa'al laah; wa kullun atawhu daakhireen

1.arabic: وترى الجبال تحسبها جامدة وهي تمر مر السحاب ۚ صنع الله الذي أتقن كل شيء ۚ إنه خبير بما تفعلون
1.english: And you see the mountains, thinking them rigid, while they will pass as the passing of clouds. [It is] the work of Allah, who perfected all things. Indeed, He is Acquainted with that which you do.
1.transliteration: Wa taral jibaala tahsabuhaa jaamidatanw wa hiya tamurru marras sahaab; sun'al laahil lazeee atqana kulla shai'; innahoo khabeerum bimaa taf'aloon

1.arabic: من جاء بالحسنة فله خير منها وهم من فزع يومئذ آمنون
1.english: Whoever comes [at Judgement] with a good deed will have better than it, and they, from the terror of that Day, will be safe.
1.transliteration: Man jaaa'a bilhasanati falahoo khairum minhaa wa hum min faza'iny Yawma'izin aaminoon

1.arabic: ومن جاء بالسيئة فكبت وجوههم في النار هل تجزون إلا ما كنتم تعملون
1.english: And whoever comes with an evil deed - their faces will be overturned into the Fire, [and it will be said], "Are you recompensed except for what you used to do?"
1.transliteration: Wa man jaaa'a bissai yi'ati fakubbat wujoohuhum fin Naari hal tujzawna illaa maa kuntum ta'maloon

1.arabic: إنما أمرت أن أعبد رب هذه البلدة الذي حرمها وله كل شيء ۖ وأمرت أن أكون من المسلمين
1.english: [Say, O Muhammad], "I have only been commanded to worship the Lord of this city, who made it sacred and to whom [belongs] all things. And I am commanded to be of the Muslims [those who submit to Allah]
1.transliteration: Innamaaa umirtu an a'buda Rabba haazihil baldatil lazee harramahaa wa lahoo kullu shai'inw wa umirtu an akoona minal muslimeen

1.arabic: وأن أتلو القرآن ۖ فمن اهتدى فإنما يهتدي لنفسه ۖ ومن ضل فقل إنما أنا من المنذرين
1.english: And to recite the Qur'an." And whoever is guided is only guided for [the benefit of] himself; and whoever strays - say, "I am only [one] of the warners."
1.transliteration: Wa an atluwal Qur-aana famanih tadaa fa innnamaa yahtadee linafsihee wa man dalla faqul innamaaa ana minal munzireen

1.arabic: وقل الحمد لله سيريكم آياته فتعرفونها ۚ وما ربك بغافل عما تعملون
1.english: And say, "[All] praise is [due] to Allah. He will show you His signs, and you will recognize them. And your Lord is not unaware of what you do."
1.transliteration: Wa qulil hamdu lillaahi sa yureekum Aayaatihee fata'ri foonahaa; wa maa Rabbuka bighaaflin 'ammaa ta'maloon
